subsection.title: Sequences
subsection.p: A sequence is a list a_1,a_2,a_3,\ldots.
It converges to L if \boxed{\lim_{n\to\infty}a_n=L.}

subsection.title: Series
subsection.p: A series is an infinite sum: \boxed{\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}a_n.}
Its partial sums are s_N=a_1+a_2+\cdots+a_N.
The series converges if \lim_{N\to\infty}s_N exists.

subsection.title: Geometric series
subsection.p: If |r|\ge1, the geometric series diverges.

subsection.title: Basic convergence facts
subsection.p: If \sum a_n converges, then \boxed{a_n\to0.}
The converse is false. The harmonic series \sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\frac1n diverges, even though \frac1n\to0.

subsection.title: Power series
subsection.p: A power series centered at a has the form \boxed{\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}c_n(x-a)^n.}
A power series usually converges only on an interval.

subsection.title: Common Taylor series
Taylor polynomial
subsection.p: The degree n Taylor polynomial of f at a is \boxed{T_n(x) = f(a) + f'(a)(x-a) + \frac{f''(a)}{2!}(x-a)^2 + \cdots + \frac{f^{(n)}(a)}{n!}(x-a)^n.}

subsection.title: Taylor's theorem with remainder
subsection.p: If f has n+1 derivatives near a, then \boxed{f(x)=T_n(x)+R_n(x).}

subsection.title: Warning
subsection.p: A Taylor polynomial is always a local approximation.
A Taylor series may fail to equal the original function, even when the function has derivatives of all orders.

subsection.title: Multivariable connection
subsection.p: The one-variable approximation f(a+h)\approx f(a)+f'(a)h becomes f(\mathbf p+\mathbf h) \approx f(\mathbf p)+Df_{\mathbf p}(\mathbf h).

The one-variable quadratic approximation f(a+h)\approx f(a)+f'(a)h+\frac12 f''(a)h^2 becomes f(\mathbf p+\mathbf h) \approx f(\mathbf p) + Df_{\mathbf p}(\mathbf h) + \frac12\mathbf h^T H_f(\mathbf p)\mathbf h.
